description_long: This sculpture depicts the Hindu deity Ganesha, characterized by an elephant head with a long, curved trunk and large ears. The figure is seated cross-legged, with detailed ornamentation on the headdress, arms, and torso, including jewelry and draped garments. The hands are positioned in traditional mudras, one holding an object and another resting on the knee. The surface has a textured, matte finish with visible sculpting marks, giving it a handcrafted appearance. The back of the sculpture shows the continuation of the headdress and garment folds, with a braided element running down the center. The overall coloration is a warm, earthy brown, resembling terracotta or clay.
description: A detailed, earthy brown sculpture of the Hindu deity Ganesha, seated cross-legged with ornate features and a textured, handcrafted finish.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing Ganesha's face, trunk, and detailed ornamentation on the chest and arms.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the trunk, ear, and layered garment folds.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the headdress, braided element, and draped clothing.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the opposite profile of the trunk, ear, and garment details.
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate a specific setting.
